pr.title: Fix itbox issues, mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2204 updates the box item translation unit, changes itbox.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, restores or repositions the item state table in itbox.c, changes spawned box cleanup from efLib_DestroyAll to grKongo_801D8058, adjusts includes accordingly, and tightens the MAX_ROT_VEL float literal. The bot report records one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox and a small .sdata2 improvement.

key_files.role: Primary source change: item state table placement/addition, cleanup helper correction, include swap, and MAX_ROT_VEL literal adjustment.
Marks melee/it/items/itbox.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.

what_changed.text: itbox.c removed the ef/eflib.h include and added gr/grkongo.h.
ItemStateTable it_803F5850[] appears near the top of itbox.c with entries for itBox_UnkMotion0 through itBox_UnkMotion8 handlers.
itBox_Logic1_Spawned and itBox_Logic1_Destroyed were moved after it_80286088 in the shown diff.
itBox_Logic1_Destroyed now calls grKongo_801D8058 on ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj instead of efLib_DestroyAll, then clears spawned_gobj to NULL.

The decomp bot reported linked code +5072 bytes, matched data +144 bytes, linked data +264 bytes, one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox from 0.00% to 100.00%, and .sdata2 improvement from 94.12% to 98.04%.

smart_moves.text: The PR pairs the configure.py matching-state change with a bot report showing a new itbox .data match, rather than only changing source without recorded progress evidence.
The cleanup-helper change also updates the include surface from ef/eflib.h to gr/grkongo.h, keeping includes aligned with the call actually used.
The small MAX_ROT_VEL literal correction is narrow and is accompanied by a bot-reported .sdata2 improvement.

decomp_lessons.text: For item files with state tables, declaration placement and data ownership can be part of reaching a data match; in this PR, adding or moving ItemStateTable it_803F5850[] is associated with a bot-reported .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox.
Small floating-point literal precision changes can affect .sdata2 matching; here MAX_ROT_VEL changed by a very small amount and the bot reported .sdata2 improvement for itbox.
When a helper call changes subsystem ownership, update the corresponding include rather than leaving a stale include from the old helper.

naming_conventions.text: The PR keeps address-style names such as it_803F5850 and it_80286088 where semantics are not fully documented in the slice, consistent with conservative naming.
Local item pointer naming uses ip for Item* recovered from GET_ITEM(gobj), a short role name already present in the changed functions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Marking itbox.c as Matching was done alongside source/data changes and a bot report showing a new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox.
The PR uses exact literal tuning for MAX_ROT_VEL, likely to affect floating constant placement or value matching; the only direct evidence is the diff plus bot-reported .sdata2 improvement.
The PR replaces efLib_DestroyAll with grKongo_801D8058 for spawned_gobj cleanup in itBox_Logic1_Destroyed, suggesting helper identity was important for the correct match or behavior in this file.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot report for GALE01 recorded linked code +0.13% (+5072 bytes), matched data +0.01% (+144 bytes), linked data +0.02% (+264 bytes), one new itbox .data match, and one itbox .sdata2 improvement.

searchable_terms: Fix itbox issues, mark as matching
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where ItemStateTable declaration placement affected .data matching.
Search for grKongo_801D8058 callsites to understand when item spawned_gobj cleanup should use grKongo instead of efLib_DestroyAll.
Search for MAX_ROT_VEL or 0.05235987715423107 to see whether this exact literal is reused in related physics code.
Review current itbox.c to confirm whether it_803F5850 ownership and matching status remained stable after PR 2204.

notes: Diff and changed-file metadata are complete for this slice.
PR body is empty and there are no review comments in the loaded evidence.
The bot report provides progress metrics, but the slice does not include a full build log, objdiff output, or author explanation.
Associations between individual source edits and specific report improvements are inferred from proximity in the PR, not proven by per-symbol verifier output.

accepted_candidate_records.title: itbox matching PR tied configure promotion to bot-reported data progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2204 changed configure.py to mark melee/it/items/itbox.c as Matching. The bot report for the same PR recorded one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itbox, from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus a .sdata2 improvement from 94.12% to 98.04%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: itbox destroyed cleanup uses grKongo helper in this PR
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2204, itBox_Logic1_Destroyed changed spawned_gobj cleanup from efLib_DestroyAll(ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj) to grKongo_801D8058(ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj), and the include changed from ef/eflib.h to gr/grkongo.h.

source_update_candidates.title: itbox.c matching promotion in PR 2204
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2204 promoted melee/it/items/itbox.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py after changes in src/melee/it/items/itbox.c; the bot report recorded main/melee/it/items/itbox .data at 100.00% after the PR.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be useful as a path-specific historical fact, but should be curated against current configure.py and current matching reports before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: itBox_Logic1_Destroyed cleanup helper
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2204 changed itBox_Logic1_Destroyed to call grKongo_801D8058 for ip->xDD4_itemVar.box.spawned_gobj cleanup instead of efLib_DestroyAll, and updated includes accordingly.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be a reusable path-specific cleanup fact, but the slice does not explain why grKongo_801D8058 is semantically correct.

source_update_candidates.title: Exact itbox MAX_ROT_VEL literal observed in matching PR
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2204 changed MAX_ROT_VEL in itbox.c to 0.05235987715423107 and the bot report recorded a .sdata2 improvement for main/melee/it/items/itbox.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential literal/value note; should be verified against current source and data ownership before adding to any data sheet.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from this slice that all spawned_gobj cleanup should use grKongo_801D8058; the evidence is limited to itBox_Logic1_Destroyed.
Do not treat the bot report as proof that every individual source edit was necessary; it only reports aggregate PR progress.
Do not promote a global rule about moving state tables before functions; this PR only shows one itbox case.
